candidate_a.title: Interactive Communal Meal Assembler
candidate_a.target_user: First-year apartment dwellers with culinary novice skills
candidate_a.problem_or_desire: Desire for social shared meals to overcome urban isolation
candidate_a.core_concept: Collaborative cooking experience between remote users.
candidate_a.proposed_approach: A guided, synchronous cooking session where two users prepare complementary parts of a meal via video call.
candidate_a.prototype_scope: Host 5 pairs of users in a guided 'cook-along' where they prepare different components of a shared meal simultaneously. | Design a two-part recipe; Recruit 5 pairs of participants; Facilitate 5 live sessions; Measure social satisfaction

candidate_b.title: Interactive Communal Meal Assembler
candidate_b.target_user: First-year apartment dwellers with culinary novice skills
candidate_b.problem_or_desire: Desire for social shared meals to overcome urban isolation
candidate_b.core_concept: Collaborative modular kit prep through synchronized smart hardware to connect solo kitchens
candidate_b.proposed_approach: Supplying meal sets that require coordination with someone else's smart stove to complete temperature thresholds for dishes to unite
candidate_b.prototype_scope: One physical joint appliance prototype where users at separate physical desks coordinate timed cooking commands to finalize two collaborative bowls

candidate_a.title: StudioPulse
candidate_a.problem_or_desire: Difficulty calculating the correct time allowance for revisions vs. design production
candidate_a.core_concept: Predictive duration simulator based on style complexity benchmarks
candidate_a.proposed_approach: Questionnaire interface that asks users to input dimensions, density of elements, and file type requirements
candidate_a.prototype_scope: An interactive form calculating an estimated project hour count based on toggle choices of design project type

candidate_b.title: StudioPulse
candidate_b.problem_or_desire: Difficulty calculating the correct time allowance for revisions vs. design production
candidate_b.core_concept: Predictive duration simulator based on project parameters.
candidate_b.proposed_approach: A calculator that allows designers to input project variables and receive a rough estimate based on their own historical data.
candidate_b.prototype_scope: A web form that outputs a time estimate based on user-defined multipliers. | Define 5-10 project variables; Build a form to collect these variables; Implement a simple calculation logic

candidate_a.title: Post-Coffee Circadian Monitoring
candidate_a.problem_or_desire: Overdose on caffeine causing unintended interference with evening sleep and focus patterns
candidate_a.core_concept: Optimizing caffeine intake based on individual sleep readiness.
candidate_a.proposed_approach: A service where users manually input their sleep data from a wearable, and receive a daily recommendation for their coffee roast and amount.
candidate_a.prototype_scope: A 10-person pilot where users report sleep data and receive a daily email recommendation. | Recruit 10 users with wearables.; Set up a manual email system for daily recommendations.; Run the pilot for 2 weeks.

candidate_b.title: Post-Coffee Circadian Monitoring
candidate_b.problem_or_desire: Overdose on caffeine causing unintended interference with evening sleep and focus patterns
candidate_b.core_concept: Optimizing roast and amount delivered based on real-time sleep readiness tracker integration
candidate_b.proposed_approach: Interface querying wearable physiological sensors (HEV or similar devices) to curate caffeine volume/roast lightened levels each day before purchase/dispensing
candidate_b.prototype_scope: Beta software connecting current fitness sensor sleep summaries with a personalized daily intake delivery recommendation chart sent daily via manual email dispatch

candidate_a.title: Shadow Script
candidate_a.problem_or_desire: Stalled imagination due to lack of character stakes
candidate_a.core_concept: Reverse Choose-Your-Own-Adventure where the player creates obstacles for an AI.
candidate_a.proposed_approach: A narrative game where the player chooses obstacles for a pre-scripted character, and the game displays the resulting outcome.
candidate_a.prototype_scope: Two scripted chapters with one 'conflict injection' scene that branches into two possible outcomes. | Write two chapters and two outcomes; Implement basic branching logic; Build simple UI for choice selection

candidate_b.title: Shadow Script
candidate_b.problem_or_desire: Stalled imagination due to lack of character stakes
candidate_b.core_concept: A 'reverse Choose-Your-Own-Adventure' where the player constructs the obstacle/protagonist and then attempts to break the story outcome for a sidekick AI.
candidate_b.proposed_approach: Utilize prompt-input dynamics where user-led conflicts dictate probability curves that the machine tries to solve during session rounds.
candidate_b.prototype_scope: Two scripted chapters defining the protagonist and one 'conflict injection' scene test.

candidate_a.title: Sensory-Blind Catering Experiences
candidate_a.target_user: Office event organizers targeting high social engagement
candidate_a.problem_or_desire: Traditional group events often focus too heavily on conversation distractants over menu intentionality
candidate_a.core_concept: Blind-dining optimized menus structured entirely on sequential textural mouth-feel gradients
candidate_a.proposed_approach: Hosting managed catering that obscures visuals during the process to ensure 100% focus on tasting menus
candidate_a.prototype_scope: Catering session of six courses involving twenty participants aimed at qualitative social memory-mapping

candidate_b.title: Sensory-Blind Catering Experiences
candidate_b.target_user: Office event organizers targeting high social engagement
candidate_b.problem_or_desire: Traditional group events often focus too heavily on conversation distractants over menu intentionality
candidate_b.core_concept: Focus on taste and texture through sensory deprivation.
candidate_b.proposed_approach: A curated tasting event where participants are blindfolded to focus on flavor profiles.
candidate_b.prototype_scope: Host one 20-person event with a blindfolded tasting menu. | Design a 6-course tasting menu; Recruit 20 participants; Host the event; Collect qualitative feedback
